Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S4-ahc030-polyomino-mining
Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

Grading target:
Lower probing cost is better.
Contest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

authored_response.decision_notes: The problem requires an optimization strategy for spatial search under uncertainty and cost constraints. I am decomposing this into a search strategy component and a probabilistic estimation component.

helpers.label: search_strategy_optimizer
helpers.instructions: Develop an algorithmic strategy to minimize drilling and query costs. This should involve a decision tree or a greedy heuristic that selects the next probe location based on the known polyomino shapes, the results of previous noisy aggregate queries, and the potential information gain regarding the location of the oil fields.

helpers.label: probabilistic_field_estimator
helpers.instructions: Create a mathematical model to estimate the probability distribution of oil-containing cells across the grid. This model must take noisy aggregate query results and previous drilling data as inputs to update the likelihood of each cell being part of a specific polyomino shape, accounting for the geometric constraints of the known shapes.

authored_response.synthesis_brief: The parent agent will use the probabilistic field estimator to maintain a belief map of the oil locations. The search strategy optimizer will then use this belief map to select the most cost-effective next action (drilling or querying) until the uncertainty is low enough to make a final guess with minimal cost.

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S4-ahc030-polyomino-mining
Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

Grading target:
Lower probing cost is better.
Contest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

authored_response.decision_notes: Synthesizing child results into a final strategy for the polyomino mining problem.
authored_response.response: FINAL_ANSWER: Implement a Bayesian Search Strategy using a Probabilistic Field Estimator and a Search Strategy Optimizer. The estimator maintains a belief map P(S) of oil locations by updating the probability of each cell being part of a polyomino shape based on drilling results and noisy aggregate queries using the formula P(S|Q, D) proportional to P(Q|S) * P(S|D). The optimizer selects the next action (drilling for high-certainty local verification or querying for global information gain) by maximizing the expected Bayesian information gain per unit cost. The process terminates when the uncertainty in the belief map is sufficiently low to allow a final guess with minimal expected error cost. JUSTIFICATION: This approach minimizes total probing cost by balancing the high cost of drilling with the lower-cost, noisy information provided by aggregate queries, using a greedy heuristic to maximize information gain per query cost.

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S4-ahc030-polyomino-mining
Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

Grading target:
Lower probing cost is better.
Contest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

message.content:
```json
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "Synthesizing child results into a final strategy for the polyomino mining problem.",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: Implement a Bayesian Search Strategy using a Probabilistic Field Estimator and a Search Strategy Optimizer. The estimator maintains a belief map $P(S)$ of oil locations by updating the probability of each cell being part of a polyomino shape based on drilling results and noisy aggregate queries using the formula $P(S|Q, D) \propto P(Q|S) \cdot P(S|D)$. The optimizer selects the next action (drilling for high-certainty local verification or querying for global information gain) by maximizing the expected Bayesian information gain per unit cost. The process terminates when the uncertainty in the belief map is sufficiently low to allow a final guess with minimal expected error cost.\n\nJUSTIFICATION: This approach minimizes total probing cost by balancing the high cost of drilling with the lower-cost, noisy information provided by aggregate queries, using a greedy heuristic to maximize information gain per query cost.",
  "result_type": "solution"
}
```

final_authored_response.response: FINAL_ANSWER: Implement a Bayesian Search Strategy using a Probabilistic Field Estimator and a Search Strategy Optimizer. The estimator maintains a belief map P(S) of oil locations by updating the probability of each cell being part of a polyomino shape based on drilling results and noisy aggregate queries using the formula P(S|Q, D) proportional to P(Q|S) * P(S|D). The optimizer selects the next action (drilling for high-certainty local verification or querying for global information gain) by maximizing the expected Bayesian information gain per unit cost. The process terminates when the uncertainty in the belief map is sufficiently low to allow a final guess with minimal expected error cost. JUSTIFICATION: This approach minimizes total probing cost by balancing the high cost of drilling with the lower-cost, noisy information provided by aggregate queries, using a greedy heuristic to maximize information gain per query cost.
